title: DungeonTester: Wilderland Liberation
blurb: Oregon Trail–style comic frames. An original thrall-to-liberator arc through the Wilderland — warrants, orcs, and Candlemire’s collar yards. Inspired by Django’s beat structure; no film or LOTR trademark names.

encounterCadence.note: Engine-side: roll next random ambush after 10–20 frames advance; prefer chapter enemyThemes.

chapters.title: Chain-Road Dawn
chapters.tagline: A numbered thrall is bought off a road of cages — and asked to kill for paper freedom.

chapters.title: Dust and Debt
chapters.tagline: Warrants pay in silver and scars while Quill teaches steel that answers to no collar.

chapters.title: The Wanted Mark
chapters.tagline: A carved brand leads east; friends and hunters argue over who owns the man who escaped.

chapters.title: River of Brands
chapters.tagline: Barges haul branded cargo across a mud-brown river the freeholds pretend not to see.

chapters.title: Candlemire Gates
chapters.tagline: Lord Cade's border keeps smile for coin and iron for mercy; Lyra is somewhere past the smoke.

chapters.title: House of Collars
chapters.tagline: Guest rooms overlook yards of rings and numbers; friendship here is whispered at risk of skin.

chapters.title: Blood Mandolin
chapters.tagline: Candlemire's sport pits fighters under lanterns while a mandolin keeps time with screams.

chapters.title: Liberation March
chapters.tagline: Cages open. Brands break. The field that grew cane learns a different crop: uprising.

chapters.title: Free Horizon
chapters.tagline: Roads west remember every foot that left a collar behind — and ask what freedom costs next.

nodes.title: Cage Line
nodes.body: Mist sits on the Chain-Road like damp wool. Iron cages rattle on ox-wains while overseers count numbers instead of names. You are Nine-Mark — thrall, fighter, and cargo.

nodes.title: Chain-Road Dawn: Quiet Counsel
nodes.body: Wind carries iron smell farther than voices. Tracks braid, unbraid, and lie with professional cheer. Continue is the only honest verb left.

nodes.title: Chain-Road Dawn: Road Hygiene
nodes.body: Bootleather remembers every mile better than maps do. Memory of cages arrives uninvited, then leaves when named. Another frame turns like a dusty page.

nodes.title: Chain-Road Dawn: Old Hunger
nodes.body: Crows argue over something dead you hope is not destiny. Somewhere a horn asks a question steel usually answers. The Wilderland watches and declines to explain itself.

nodes.title: Chain-Road Dawn: Freemark Thoughts
nodes.body: Quill hums a tune that never finishes the same way twice. Ash keeps counsel; Quill keeps a ledger of silences. Steel rests light; resolve does not.

nodes.title: Chain-Road Dawn: Ledger Weather
nodes.body: Clouds drag shadows across cane-colored grass. Hope and suspicion share the same waterskin. Somewhere east, a ledger page waits to be rewritten.

nodes.title: Chain-Road Dawn: Steel Practice
nodes.body: A creek argues with stones like a tired magistrate. Rations taste like postponement and pepper. Quill nods toward the darker horizon without ceremony.

nodes.title: The Binder Arrives
nodes.body: A lean man in travel-stained grey tips his hat to the overseer as if haggling over mule feed. Halbrecht Quill — warrant binder, chain-buyer, and something like a scholar of ugly laws. He pays coin for your papers without looking away from your eyes.

nodes.title: Chain-Road Dawn: Night Watch
nodes.body: Night insects keep score of every secret step. The road offers three bad choices and one worse delay. You walk on because stopping is how numbers return.

nodes.title: Chain-Road Dawn: Dust Mile
nodes.body: Dust lifts off the track in pale sheets. You check the freemark and find it still yours. The next mile does not apologize.

nodes.title: Paper Freedom
nodes.body: Quill slides a stamped sheet across the wagon board. Freedom on parchment, conditional on service: hunt the guilty Quill names, keep your steel sharp, and ask no soft questions. He waits for your word.

choices.label: Take the warrant path
choices.approach: Accept conditional freedom; work for Quill.

outcome.text: Quill smiles without warmth. “Good. The road hates empty hands.”

choices.label: Bargain for a name first
choices.approach: Ask whose name this work is really for.

success.text: He softens half a degree. “Lyra. Sold east years ago. Candlemire keeps pretty ledgers.”

fail.text: Quill’s face closes. “Earn the right to ask.” He still pushes the papers toward you.

choices.label: Test the man's honesty
choices.approach: Watch for a twitch that says he buys thralls for worse work.

success.text: No twitch — only tired calculation. He is dangerous and, for now, aligned.

fail.text: You misread courtesy for cruelty. Quill shrugs and hands you a short sword anyway.

nodes.title: First Mile Free
nodes.body: Boots that never chose a direction now choose west, then south, at Quill’s nudge. Crows watch the cages dwindle. Freedom feels like hunger with better manners.

nodes.title: Orc Chainers
nodes.body: Three chain-orcs peel from the pine edge, still smelling of the cages they sold this morning. They want their property returned. Quill calmly loads a crossbow and says, “Show them the paperwork is yours.”

choices.label: Stand and fight
choices.approach: Use hotbar steel; clocks stay off — take your time.

outcome.text: Chain-Orc Reclaimer falls. The road tastes like iron and relief.

choices.label: Outwit the terrain
choices.approach: Use cover, noise, and Quill’s nasty little tricks.

success.text: The ambush becomes your board. They break first.

fail.text: Clever turns loud. You bleed for the lesson and win anyway.

choices.label: Break past and run
choices.approach: Freedom first; honor later.

outcome.text: Dust and speed. Quill calls it strategic cowardice — fondly.

nodes.title: Quill's Lesson
nodes.body: “Steel answers collar-law better than ink,” Quill says, wiping ichor from the bolt. He drills stance, breath, and the difference between killing for a warrant and killing for rage. You practice until dusk makes the road one color.

nodes.title: Campfire Question
nodes.body: Night fire snaps. Quill boils bitter tea and asks what you will do if Lyra is already broken past rescue. The question sits between you like a third traveler.

choices.label: I bring her home anyway
choices.approach: Refuse the premise that anyone is past saving.

outcome.text: Quill nods once. “Then we plan for stubborn miracles.”

choices.label: Then Candlemire pays in blood
choices.approach: If she is gone, Cade Mire still owns the debt.

outcome.text: Quill’s eyes glitter. “Justice with teeth. I can work with that.”

choices.label: I will decide when I see her
choices.approach: Keep counsel until the yard is real.

outcome.text: “Wise or evasive — same coin until spent,” Quill murmurs.

nodes.title: Vern Cornered
nodes.body: Vern sits against a tree with a leg broken by his own horse. He begs for Cade’s protection and offers a torn ledger page with women’s names. Lyra’s sits three lines down, sold as “song-thrall, unbroken.”

choices.label: Bind him for trial
choices.approach: Alive for Quill’s warrant and a clean conscience.

outcome.text: Vern weeps gratitude he does not deserve. The ledger page is yours.

choices.label: End him for Lowhedge
choices.approach: Justice for ash roofs; take the page either way.

outcome.text: The forest swallows the sound. Quill files the warrant stamped CLOSED.

choices.label: Press for Candlemire routes
choices.approach: Pain or promise — get the eastern road correct.

success.text: Vern sketches fords and bribe posts. “Cade smiles when he sells people,” he spits.

fail.text: Vern faints before finishing. You still have the ledger shred and a headache.

nodes.title: Name on Paper
nodes.body: You say Lyra aloud until the vowels stop shaking. Quill packs Vern’s routes — or what remains of them — and points toward a freeman town that stamps men as unpaid labor instead of numbered iron. Dawn smells like pine and unfinished debts.

nodes.title: Freemark Bridge
nodes.body: Freemark’s bridge exacts a toll in stories, not coins. You tell enough of Chain-Road to pass, and hide enough of Candlemire to sleep. Quill buys ink, ball, and powder like a man preparing a small war.

nodes.title: Which Warrant?
nodes.body: Three warrants hang like wet laundry: a goblin raid-chief, a barge cutter on the Brand-River feeder, and a knight who sold villagers under Cade’s quiet seal. Quill lets you choose the first debt to collect.

choices.label: Hunt the goblin chief
choices.approach: Clear hills so freefolk stop paying ‘protection.’

outcome.text: Quill packs snares. “Hills first. Rivers remember.”

choices.label: Hunt the barge cutter
choices.approach: Follow water toward Candlemire’s quiet supply.

outcome.text: “Rivers teach patience,” Quill says, already smelling mud.

choices.label: Hunt the selling knight
choices.approach: Strike the soft armor of Cade’s respectables.

outcome.text: Quill’s smile thins. “Careful. Knights have friends with seals.”

nodes.title: Free or Follow?
nodes.body: You can cut canvas tonight and spill rings into mud — noisy mercy — or track the barge to its Candlemire drop and learn routes. Quill’s face is a hung jury.

choices.label: Cut the canvas; free who you can
choices.approach: Immediate liberation; accept the horn that follows.

outcome.text: Mud takes chains; alarms take the night. You run with wet lungs and cleaner hands.

choices.approach: Intelligence first; rescue with a map.

outcome.text: You learn landing codes and a steward’s soft knock. Quill files both.

success.text: He names Lyra’s sale lot and looks away forever.

fail.text: He takes coin and shouts anyway. Spears bloom in reed.

outcome.text: Oars bite mud. The far bank arrives colder and closer.

fail.text: Mud wins a round. Quill hauls you up coughing brands and bile.

outcome.text: The ferryman takes seals at face value. Fog forgives the lie.

outcome.text: Copper becomes welcome. Suspicion naps.

choices.label: Finish hard justice
choices.approach: Burn what remains of Cade’s legal skin.

outcome.text: Fire writes the last ledger. Names stop being inventory.

choices.label: Found a shared dawn
choices.approach: Stay and build a freemark hold that keeps no collars.

outcome.text: Hammers replace brands. Quill files a warrant against slavery itself.

nodes.title: The Merciful Road
nodes.body: West opens without a brand on any skyline. Lyra walks beside you; Quill tips his hat and files no warrant for kindness. The Wilderland keeps room for people who leave cages empty.

nodes.title: Hard Justice
nodes.body: Candlemire’s last seals blacken. Hard justice is a fire that warms freed hands and frightens soft thieves. You do not apologize for the smoke.

nodes.title: Shared Dawn
nodes.body: A freemark hold rises where cane once hid screams. Shared dawn means watches, kitchens, and laws that refuse inventory of people. Quill stays to teach binders a better trade.

endings.title: The Merciful Road
endings.blurb: Ash and Lyra leave Cade's fall to other hunters and walk west under open sky.

endings.title: Hard Justice
endings.blurb: Candlemire burns by Ash's hand; the Wilderland remembers the fire longer than the names.

endings.title: Shared Dawn
endings.blurb: Quill, Ash, and the freed march forge a freehold that keeps no brands and no guests with chains.

encounterCadence.note: Engine-side: roll next random ambush after 10–20 frames advance; prefer chapter enemyThemes.
